seoTitle: How to Import Jira Issues From CSV Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to bulk import issues into Jira from a CSV file, map spreadsheet columns to Jira fields, and validate the data before importing.
intro: Re-keying a huge backlog of tasks one by one when you're migrating from a spreadsheet just isn't practical. This guide covers structuring a CSV file, mapping its columns to Jira fields, and running the import safely.

steps.heading: Prepare your CSV file
steps.body: Format your spreadsheet with proper commas, quote markers, and a mandatory summary title header before uploading.

steps.heading: Launch the import wizard
steps.body: From your project, go to the filters header and select the Import From CSV command to open the upload window.

steps.heading: Select your file and destination project
steps.body: Choose your prepared CSV document, click Next, then select the destination project and verify the date formats look correct.

steps.heading: Map fields and values
steps.body: Match your CSV column headers to Jira fields like Summary and Description, then map raw text values to native issue type options.

steps.heading: Validate and run the import
steps.body: Click Validate to check for formatting errors, then click Begin Import and return to your board to watch the new issues populate.

faq.question: How do I import issues into Jira from a CSV file?
faq.answer: Go to the filters header, select Import From CSV, choose your file, map your columns to Jira fields, validate, and begin the import.

faq.question: What fields are required in a CSV file for a Jira import?
faq.answer: At minimum you need a summary title header, and your file should use consistent commas and quote markers for clean parsing.

faq.question: Can I preview errors before completing a Jira CSV import?
faq.answer: Yes, the Validate step checks your file rows for formatting errors before you confirm the import.

faq.question: Does importing from CSV create duplicate issues?
faq.answer: The import process is designed to bulk-create new cards without generating duplicate project keys, as long as your data is mapped correctly.
